steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows "palladium" and must form a noun phrase that is the object of "leverage." The sentence describes nanoengineers leveraging something belonging to nanoparticles — their properties. The blank must correctly show that multiple nanoparticles possess properties.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — nanoparticles properties: No possessive marker. "Nanoparticles properties" is not grammatical — there is no apostrophe to show ownership. ✗
  • Choice B — nanoparticle's properties: Singular possessive. The sentence refers to nanoparticles (plural), so the singular "nanoparticle's" is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — nanoparticles' properties': Double possessive — there is a stray apostrophe after "properties," which is not a possessive noun here. ✗
  • Choice D — nanoparticles' properties: Plural possessive formed correctly: add an apostrophe after the final "s" of a plural noun ending in "s." ✓
That is the correct answer.
